indicators.name: CO2 emissions (total, excl. LULUCF) (Mt CO2e)
indicators.description: Carbon dioxide emissions from all sectors excluding land use, land-use change, and forestry, measured in megatons of CO2 equivalent (AR5 methodology).

indicators.name: Total greenhouse gas emissions per capita (t CO2e/capita)
indicators.description: Total greenhouse gas emissions per person excluding land use, land-use change, and forestry, in tonnes of CO2 equivalent (AR5 methodology).

indicators.name: Total greenhouse gas emissions excl. LULUCF (Mt CO2e)
indicators.description: Aggregate greenhouse gas emissions from all sectors excluding land use, land-use change, and forestry, in megatons of CO2 equivalent (AR5 methodology).

indicators.name: Terrestrial and marine protected areas (% of total territorial area)
indicators.description: Proportion of a country's total territorial area — including both land and exclusive economic zone — that is under formal protection.

indicators.name: Droughts, floods, extreme temperatures (% of population, average)
indicators.description: Average share of the population affected annually by droughts, floods, or extreme temperature events, reflecting climate-related disaster exposure.

indicators.name: Annual freshwater withdrawals, total (% of internal resources)
indicators.description: Total freshwater withdrawn for agriculture, industry, and municipal use as a percentage of a country's total internal renewable freshwater resources.

indicators.name: Access to electricity (% of population)
indicators.description: Percentage of the total population with access to electricity, including both on-grid and off-grid connections.

indicators.name: Renewable energy consumption (% of total final energy consumption)
indicators.description: Share of renewable energy — including solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, and modern biofuels — in the country's total final energy consumption.

indicators.name: Renewable electricity output (% of total electricity output)
indicators.description: Electricity generated from renewable sources — hydropower, wind, solar, geothermal, and biomass — as a share of total electricity production.

indicators.name: Energy use (kg of oil equivalent per capita)
indicators.description: Per-capita energy consumption across all sectors, expressed in kilograms of oil equivalent, covering primary energy from all sources.

indicators.name: Forest area (sq. km)
indicators.description: Land area covered by natural or planted forests of more than 0.5 hectares with tree canopy cover exceeding 10%, in square kilometers.

indicators.name: Forest area (% of land area)
indicators.description: Forest area as a percentage of total land area, used to track deforestation trends and land-use change over time.

indicators.name: Agricultural land (% of land area)
indicators.description: Share of total land area classified as arable, under permanent crops, or permanent pasture, indicating the extent of land dedicated to food production.

indicators.name: Food production index (2014-2016 = 100)
indicators.description: Aggregate measure of food crop production relative to the 2014–2016 base period, covering all edible food crops excluding feed crops.

indicators.name: Population, total
indicators.description: Total resident population, including all nationals and foreign residents, as estimated by the World Bank for mid-year.

indicators.name: Population growth (annual %)
indicators.description: Annual rate of population change, reflecting the net effect of births, deaths, and international migration.

indicators.name: Urban population (% of total population)
indicators.description: Share of the total population living in urban areas, as defined by national statistical offices.

indicators.name: Life expectancy at birth, total (years)
indicators.description: Average number of years a newborn would live if prevailing age-specific mortality rates throughout life remained constant.

indicators.name: GDP (current US$)
indicators.description: Gross domestic product at purchaser's prices in current US dollars, representing the total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country.

indicators.name: GDP per capita (current US$)
indicators.description: Gross domestic product divided by mid-year population in current US dollars, a standard measure of economic output per person.

indicators.name: GDP growth (annual %)
indicators.description: Annual percentage growth rate of GDP at market prices, measured in constant local currency and adjusted for inflation.

indicators.name: Poverty headcount ratio at $3.00 a day (2021 PPP) (% of population)
indicators.description: Percentage of the population living on less than $3.00 per day at 2021 purchasing power parity, the international (extreme) poverty line.

indicators.name: Mortality rate, under-5 (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.description: Probability that a child born in a given year will die before reaching age five, expressed per 1,000 live births.

indicators.name: People using safely managed drinking water services (% of population)
indicators.description: Share of the population using drinking water from an improved source that is accessible on premises, available when needed, and free from contamination.

indicators.name: People using at least basic sanitation services (% of population)
indicators.description: Percentage of the population with access to improved sanitation facilities that are not shared with other households.

indicators.name: Individuals using the Internet (% of population)
indicators.description: Percentage of individuals who used the Internet in the last three months, regardless of device or connection type.

indicators.name: Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people)
indicators.description: Number of active mobile phone subscriptions per 100 inhabitants, including both postpaid and prepaid accounts.

indicators.name: Access to electricity, rural (% of rural population)
indicators.description: Percentage of the rural population with access to electricity, capturing the urban-rural energy access gap.
